channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Inference-time Alignment via Sparse Junction Steering
item.description: arXiv:2602.21215v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Token-level steering has emerged as a pivotal approach for inference-time alignment, enabling fine grained control over large language models by modulating their output distributions without parameter updates. While effective, existing methods rely on...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment, alignment, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EQ-5D Classification Using Biomedical Entity-Enriched Pre-trained Language Models and Multiple Instance Learning
item.description: arXiv:2602.21216v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The EQ-5D (EuroQol 5-Dimensions) is a standardized instrument for the evaluation of health-related quality of life. In health economics, systematic literature reviews (SLRs) depend on the correct identification of publications that use the EQ-5D, but ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, experiment, attention, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Applied Sociolinguistic AI for Community Development (ASA-CD): A New Scientific Paradigm for Linguistically-Grounded Social Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2602.21217v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper establishes Applied Sociolinguistic AI for Community Development (ASA-CD) as a novel scientific paradigm for addressing community challenges through linguistically grounded, AI-enabled intervention. ASA-CD introduces three key contributions...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, RAG, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Field-Theoretic Memory for AI Agents: Continuous Dynamics for Context Preservation
item.description: arXiv:2602.21220v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a memory system for AI agents that treats stored information as continuous fields governed by partial differential equations rather than discrete entries in a database. The approach draws from classical field theory: memories diffuse throug...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, retrieval, memory, arxiv, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Beyond Refusal: Probing the Limits of Agentic Self-Correction for Semantic Sensitive Information
item.description: arXiv:2602.21496v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While defenses for structured PII are mature, Large Language Models (LLMs) pose a new threat: Semantic Sensitive Information (SemSI), where models infer sensitive identity attributes, generate reputation-harmful content, or hallucinate potentially wro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, GPT, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ARLArena: A Unified Framework for Stable Agentic Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2602.21534v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic reinforcement learning (ARL) has rapidly gained attention as a promising paradigm for training agents to solve complex, multi-step interactive tasks. Despite encouraging early results, ARL remains highly unstable, often leading to training col...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, study, attention, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Power and Limitations of Aggregation in Compound AI Systems
item.description: arXiv:2602.21556v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When designing compound AI systems, a common approach is to query multiple copies of the same model and aggregate the responses to produce a synthesized output. Given the homogeneity of these models, this raises the question of whether aggregation unl...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, LLM, prompt, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ProactiveMobile: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Boosting Proactive Intelligence on Mobile Devices
item.description: arXiv:2602.21858v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have made significant progress in mobile agent development, yet their capabilities are predominantly confined to a reactive paradigm, where they merely execute explicit user commands. The emerging paradigm of p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, LLM, GPT, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ACAR: Adaptive Complexity Routing for Multi-Model Ensembles with Auditable Decision Traces
item.description: arXiv:2602.21231v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present ACAR (Adaptive Complexity and Attribution Routing), a measurement framework for studying multi-model orchestration under auditable conditions. ACAR uses self-consistency variance (sigma) computed from N=3 probe samples to route tasks across...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, arxiv, LLM, GPT, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Urban Vibrancy Embedding and Application on Traffic Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2602.21232v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Urban vibrancy reflects the dynamic human activity within urban spaces and is often measured using mobile data that captures floating population trends. This study proposes a novel approach to derive Urban Vibrancy embeddings from real-time floating p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, arxiv, study, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Group Orthogonalized Policy Optimization:Group Policy Optimization as Orthogonal Projection in Hilbert Space
item.description: arXiv:2602.21269v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present Group Orthogonalized Policy Optimization (GOPO), a new alignment algorithm for large language models derived from the geometry of Hilbert function spaces. Instead of optimizing on the probability simplex and inheriting the exponential curva...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, reasoning, alignment, experiment, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: fEDM+: A Risk-Based Fuzzy Ethical Decision Making Framework with Principle-Level Explainability and Pluralistic Validation
item.description: arXiv:2602.21746v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In a previous work, we introduced the fuzzy Ethical Decision-Making framework (fEDM), a risk-based ethical reasoning architecture grounded in fuzzy logic. The original model combined a fuzzy Ethical Risk Assessment module (fERA) with ethical decision ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, reasoning, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Uncertainty-Aware Diffusion Model for Multimodal Highway Trajectory Prediction via DDIM Sampling
item.description: arXiv:2602.21319v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurate and uncertainty-aware trajectory prediction remains a core challenge for autonomous driving, driven by complex multi-agent interactions, diverse scene contexts and the inherently stochastic nature of future motion. Diffusion-based generative ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, experiment, model, multimodal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Neural network optimization strategies and the topography of the loss landscape
item.description: arXiv:2602.21276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural networks are trained by optimizing multi-dimensional sets of fitting parameters on non-convex loss landscapes. Low-loss regions of the landscapes correspond to the parameter sets that perform well on the training data. A key issue in machine le...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, tool, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
